issue_title: Add the reusable podcast canvas editor
issue_body:
Build the first usable canvas editor for customizing a selected podcast style into a reusable show layout. The editor should let creators open the chosen preset as a starting point, adjust visible layout elements such as speaker frames, captions, title text, background, and overlay areas, and save the result as a named show template. This is the next foundation because the product already has episode setup and preset choice, but it still lacks the Canva-like direct manipulation workspace that makes styles personal and reusable.

## Acceptance (definition of done)
A reviewer can create or load an episode setup, choose a preset style, open a canvas editor from that style, visibly customize the layout elements without manually editing code, save the design as a named reusable show template, and see that saved template available for future episode use. The existing verification command `node scripts/run-tests.mjs` passes and includes coverage for opening the canvas editor, changing at least one layout element, saving a template, and reselecting it.

---
This is the current active step for this product. The maintainer will only merge a pull request that completes THIS step and demonstrably works. Open a PR that closes this issue.

_Why this step now: The completed work covers importing/setup and preset selection. The highest-value missing product capability is now personalization and reuse: creators need a visual workspace that turns presets into their own show identity before deeper editing, audio cleanup, captions, b-roll, or export can feel coherent._
